concerns.text: FR-002 mandates 'CPU-only mode' and forbids CUDA/GPU. However, US-02 Acceptance Scenario 3 sets a performance target of '60 seconds per image on a CPU-only environment'. While not a direct contradiction, the spec lacks a definition of the specific CPU hardware (e.g., core count, architecture) to make this '60 seconds' claim testable and consistent with the 'CPU-only' constraint. Without a hardware baseline, the requirement is ambiguous.

US-01 Acceptance Scenario 1 and SC-001 both specify a '≥ 90%' accuracy threshold. However, the 'Assumptions' section states the sample size is assumed to be 'adequate to achieve a statistical power of ≥ 0.8'. There is no explicit link in the text between the sample size assumption and the 90% threshold. If the sample size is insufficient, the 90% target might be unachievable not due to model failure but due to statistical noise. The spec should clarify if the 90% target is conditional on the power analysis in US-03/SC-005.

FR-006 requires evaluating reconstruction fidelity using 'SSIM and LPIPS metrics'. SC-002 states this is measured against 'the original document layout and the ground-truth "swapped" document (if available)'. The phrase '(if available)' creates a potential inconsistency: if the ground-truth swapped document is NOT available, the requirement to measure against it (as implied by the metric definition) becomes impossible to fulfill as stated. The spec should explicitly define the fallback metric or reference (e.g., 'or against the original layout if no ground-truth swap exists') to ensure the requirement is always testable.
